title: Swap return value is trusted without balance-delta validation
reason: Overlaps existing reserve-drain findings (F-001/F-005/F-009/F-010/F-018); as written it depends on a misbehaving swap router and does not add a distinct exploitable root cause.

title: Missing revert-message length guards can revert callback handlers
reason: Current protocol-generated revert messages are >=32 bytes; short-message failure requires atypical external misuse of these contracts as revert targets and does not show meaningful protocol-level exploitability.

title: `claimRefund` emits cleared values after deleting storage
reason: Informational event-quality issue only; no direct fund-loss, theft, lockup, or DoS impact.

title: Refund entry at `externalId == 0x0` is unclaimable
reason: Relies on non-standard zero externalId callback payloads; normal protocol paths derive non-zero IDs, so impact is largely self-inflicted and low practical risk.

title: GatewaySend.onCall returns incorrect success value causing callback failure
reason: Claim is based on an incorrect interface assumption and does not demonstrate an actual failing execution path in this codebase.

title: GatewaySend.onCall does not validate fromToken transfer quantity
reason: Already covered by existing amount/token trust and transfer-accounting findings (F-005/F-001).

title: GatewaySend.depositAndCall does not use swapData for output token calculation
reason: Duplicate of the existing asset-binding failure in F-010.

title: GatewaySend._doMixSwap missing validation for fromToken amount
reason: Duplicate of F-001 (attacker-controlled `fromTokenAmount` and token source mismatch).

title: GatewaySend depositAndCall lacks slippage protection for swap output
reason: Slippage constraints are user-specified in swap params (`minReturnAmount`); this is not a distinct protocol security bug.

title: GatewayTransferNative.onCall fee calculation uses input amount not output amount
reason: Economic/fee-policy concern, not a standalone exploit enabling theft, insolvency, lockup, or permissionless DoS.

title: GatewayTransferNative withdraw function is public not internal
reason: Already captured in accumulated finding F-008.

title: GatewayTransferNative.withdrawToNativeChain allows arbitrary recipient addresses without validation
reason: Receiver bytes are intentionally user-provided for multi-chain formats; invalid values are user-input risk and already covered where truncation causes loss (F-013/F-003).

title: GatewayCrossChain._swapAndSendERC20Tokens calculates amountInMax with slippage on quote not actual
reason: Standard exact-output quoting behavior; no distinct exploit beyond already captured allowance/residual and liveness issues.

title: GatewayCrossChain onCall does not validate message length before decoding
reason: Malformed payloads revert; no credible permissionless protocol-harm path beyond rejecting invalid input.

title: GatewayCrossChain claimRefund allows anyone to claim if walletAddress is 20 bytes and caller is bot
reason: Incorrect: for 20-byte wallet addresses payout goes to decoded receiver, not the bot caller.

title: SwapDataHelperLib.decodeCompressedMixSwapParams has unchecked arithmetic in offset calculations
reason: No practical overflow/memory-corruption path shown; out-of-bounds calldata slicing reverts safely under Solidity bounds checks.
